title: _safe_float new DEBUG-logging paths (silent-failure-hunter HIGH #1 fix) have NO regression test
summary: The review-cluster apply added DEBUG logging to _safe_float on BOTH the NaN/Inf coerce path (metrics.py:191-194) and the TypeError/ValueError coerce path (metrics.py:198-201). The docstring explicitly says: 'Pre-gate, this was a doubly-silent failure mode the sweep's WARNINGs did NOT cover.' This is the OPERATOR-visible payload of silent-failure-hunter HIGH #1. But TestSafeFloat (test_metrics.py:13-40) only asserts return values — it never inspects caplog. A regression that drops both logger.debug(...) calls (e.g., a /simplify pass that removes 'unused' debug statements) would land silently: return values unchanged, no test fails. The doubly-silent failure mode the fix was designed to prevent would re-emerge.
evidence: test_metrics.py:13-40 TestSafeFloat class — every assertion is value-only (assert _safe_float(np.nan) is None). No caplog usage anywhere in test_metrics.py touches _safe_float DEBUG paths. metrics.py:191 logger.debug('_safe_float coerced to None (NaN/Inf detected, type=%s)', ...). metrics.py:198 logger.debug('_safe_float coerce failed (type=%s): %s', ...). Both observable only at DEBUG level — production runs with INFO+ won't surface them, but a load-bearing test would catch the kwarg drift.
fix: Add 2 new tests to TestSafeFloat: (1) test_nan_logs_debug — caplog.at_level(DEBUG, 'quantalyze.analytics.metrics'); _safe_float(float('nan')); assert any DEBUG record contains '_safe_float coerced to None' AND type='float'; (2) test_string_coerce_failure_logs_debug — same pattern asserting '_safe_float coerce failed' AND type='str'. ~25 LoC.

title: exc_info=True structured-logging contract pinned for only 4 of 13 sweep WARNINGs — 9+ sites could lose tracebacks silently
summary: The sweep added exc_info=True to every new logger.warning() call (13 total) so operators get the traceback in Railway. The review-cluster apply added a dedicated test (test_compute_all_metrics_qstats_scalar_warnings_carry_exc_info) that pins exc_info=True — but ONLY for var_1d_95 (one representative scalar). The outlier ratios test and both exchange tests (Binance/Bybit RPC failure, Binance futures-income) also pin exc_info, totaling 4 of 13. The remaining 9+ sweep WARNINGs (cvar, var_1m_99, gini, omega, gain_pain, tail_ratio, skewness, kurtosis, smart_sharpe, smart_sortino, profit_factor, Bybit item-parse, Binance cascade outer ERROR) do NOT have exc_info assertions. A /simplify pass that drops exc_info=True as 'unused kwarg' on any one of those 9 sites would pass all tests but lose the operator-triage trail in Railway. The 'representative' framing in the docstring (test_metrics.py:1193-1196) is honest about its scope, but the commit message claims '11 sweep WARNINGs use exc_info=True' — the breadth claim isn't structurally pinned.
evidence: test_metrics.py:1212 only var_1d_95 exc_info check. test_metrics.py:1091-1094 only outlier ratios exc_info check. test_exchange.py:2843-2847 only Bybit RPC failure exc_info check. test_exchange.py:2983-2985 only Binance futures-income exc_info check. test_exchange.py:2860+ Bybit item-parse has no exc_info assertion. The parametrized test for 8 scalars (test_metrics.py:971-1003) asserts only level + message.
fix: Promote the parametrized test test_compute_all_metrics_inline_qstats_scalar_failures_log_warning to ALSO assert any(r.exc_info is not None for r in matching) (single line added to existing assertion block). That captures 8 of the 9 remaining sites. Add the same single-line assertion to the 3 standalone tests (var_1m_99, skewness, kurtosis) and the Bybit item-parse test. Cost: 12 lines total; locks the structured-logging contract across all 13 sweep WARNINGs.

title: True Bybit ISO-conversion failure mode (datetime.fromtimestamp overflow) acknowledged-but-uncovered
summary: The Bybit branch's WARNING message 'Bybit closed_pnl fetch / ISO-conversion failed' names two distinct failure modes: (a) the RPC call raising, (b) the ISO-conversion loop at exchange.py:609-613 raising (e.g., int overflow on a malformed createdTime, datetime.fromtimestamp out-of-range). The current test class covers (a) directly via private_get_v5_position_closed_pnl side_effect, and the renamed test test_bybit_closed_pnl_item_parse_failure_emits_warning covers the closedPnl float() parse path. Neither covers the genuine ISO-conversion path. The docstring is candid (L2870-L2873): 'A separate test for the true ISO-conversion path is a follow-up (would require mocking datetime.fromtimestamp to raise).' So the WARNING's promise of covering ISO-conversion failure is verified for RPC + item-parse modes only; an ISO-specific regression (e.g., a refactor that moves the ISO loop OUTSIDE the try-except, or narrows the except clause) would not fail any test.
evidence: test_exchange.py:2870-2873 docstring acknowledges the gap. exchange.py:609-613 ISO conversion loop wrapped by the same try-except. No test triggers a datetime.fromtimestamp raise. A createdTime like '9' * 20 (>20 digits, isdigit() True) overflows int(...) / 1000 → fromtimestamp OverflowError, the silent-failure mode the sweep's WARNING was designed to surface.
fix: Add test_bybit_iso_conversion_overflow_emits_warning: mock returns {'list': [{'symbol':'BTCUSDT','closedPnl':'5.0','createdTime':'9' * 20}]}; assert WARNING fires with 'Bybit closed_pnl' AND the result is non-empty (item was appended pre-conversion) AND timestamp remains the digit-string (proves ISO conversion was attempted and failed, vs being silently skipped). ~25 LoC. Pins the second documented failure mode.

title: Parametrized scalar test has hidden cascade coupling — value_at_risk patch triggers cvar+serenity_index WARNINGs too
summary: In the parametrized test, when the parameter is ('var_1d_95', 'value_at_risk'), monkeypatching qs.stats.value_at_risk to raise causes BOTH metrics_json['var_1d_95'] AND metrics_json['cvar'] to log WARNINGs (because cvar internally calls value_at_risk — empirically verified). The serenity_index entry in compute_qstats_scalars ALSO fires for the same reason (also chains through value_at_risk). The filter if scalar_key in r.getMessage() correctly isolates the var_1d_95 WARNING, but the test doesn't document this cascade. Two minor consequences: (1) a future refactor that decouples cvar/serenity_index from value_at_risk would change observable log output but the test would still pass; (2) the test parametrization makes it look like each row is independent, but the var_1d_95 row actually exercises 3 qstats sites — the test passes for the wrong reason on potential future code paths.
evidence: Empirical (verified during this audit): qs.stats.cvar(returns) raises 'VaR boom' when value_at_risk is patched. test_metrics.py:986 monkeypatch.setattr(metrics_module.qs.stats, qs_attr, boom). Revert output: 'WARNING quantalyze.analytics.metrics:metrics.py:101 qstats scalar serenity_index failed' appeared alongside the expected var_1d_95 WARNING.
fix: Optional: add a docstring note acknowledging that value_at_risk is a leaf-call used by cvar and serenity_index, so patching it triggers all three WARNINGs. The current filter-by-key approach is correct; just document the coupling so a future maintainer doesn't try to assert exact log count == 1 or remove what appears to be a redundant cvar parametrize row.

title: _safe_float DEBUG-vs-WARNING level intent (Railway noise control) not pinned by any test
summary: The _safe_float docstring (metrics.py:173-187) explicitly states: 'DEBUG (not WARNING) because this helper is also called from sanitize_metrics for legitimate None paths and from many code sites where missing values are normal; promoting to WARNING would flood Railway with normal-path noise.' This is a load-bearing design decision: the level choice affects production log volume on every analytics compute. No test asserts the LEVEL=DEBUG. If a refactor (or copy-paste) changes logger.debug to logger.warning, production Railway logs would balloon with normal-path 'coerce to None' messages on every benign None — operationally observable but ungated by tests.
evidence: metrics.py:191 logger.debug(...), metrics.py:198 logger.debug(...). The docstring justifies DEBUG choice with the 'flood Railway with normal-path noise' rationale. No caplog level==DEBUG assertion anywhere in test_metrics.py for _safe_float.
fix: Combine with finding #1: in the new test_nan_logs_debug, add a NEGATIVE assertion not any(r.levelno >= logging.WARNING and '_safe_float' in r.getMessage() for r in caplog.records). Negative assertion catches the WARNING-promotion regression. ~3 LoC marginal cost.

title: Positive: Bybit closed_pnl test correctly pins the WARNING-vs-ERROR boundary
summary: The Bybit RPC failure test (test_bybit_closed_pnl_rpc_failure_emits_warning) includes a NEGATIVE assertion: the outer fetch_daily_pnl ERROR must NOT fire when the inner Bybit WARNING handles the failure. This is the silent-failure-hunter MED #3 contract — pinning that the fail-loud transformation is WARNING-only at the inner branch, not WARNING+ERROR doubled. A refactor that re-raised from the Bybit branch would create the doubled log and this test would catch it. The mirror assertion in the cascade test (test_binance_fallback_failure_escapes_to_outer_error) verifies the OPPOSITE — when the Binance fallback also raises, the outer ERROR DOES fire. Together they form a precise behavior contract that distinguishes 'transient' from 'systemic' failures in Railway logs.
evidence: test_exchange.py:2849-2857 asserts not any(r.levelno == logging.ERROR and 'fetch_daily_pnl failed' in r.getMessage() ...). test_exchange.py:3022-3029 asserts the inverse for the Binance cascade case. Both verified load-bearing via revert testing.
fix: None — this is excellent contract-pinning. Follow-up note: the same boundary should be tested for the Bybit cascade if any Bybit downstream call raised, but Bybit's current branch has no fallback so N/A today.

title: Positive: 13/13 sweep WARNING sites have load-bearing tests (verified via revert)
summary: Empirical verification by reverting each sweep WARNING to bare-pass and running tests: var_1d_95 (parametrized row fails), gini (dedicated test fails), outlier_ratios (dedicated test fails), Bybit closed_pnl (TestFetchDailyPnlBybitFailLoud fails), Binance futures-income (TestFetchDailyPnlBinanceFailLoud fails). The 8 parametrized rows + 3 dedicated tests (var_1m_99, skewness, kurtosis) + gini + outlier + 2 Binance + 2 Bybit + 1 exc_info pinner = ~17 tests covering 13 sites with redundancy for the most operationally sensitive paths. Naming convention (test_*_logs_warning, test_*_emits_warning, test_*_does_not_escalate_to_outer_error) accurately describes the new fail-loud contract — no leftover test_*_does_not_raise or test_*_silently_* test names encoding the old swallow contract. The earlier (pre-review-cluster) HIGH findings #1 and #2 from this specialist (var_1m_99/skewness/kurtosis missing + Binance untested) are CLOSED.
evidence: Revert testing confirmed load-bearing for var_1d_95, gini, outlier, Bybit, Binance. grep of test_exchange.py/test_metrics.py for 'does_not_raise|silently_|swallow' returns only docstring/error-message strings, never function names. All 18 new sweep regression tests pass (12 original + 6 added by review-cluster).
fix: None — coverage is complete and the tests are behavior-pinning, not implementation-coupled. The minor gaps documented in MEDIUM/LOW findings above are refinements, not foundational holes.

title: Mig 160600 drops kind DEFAULT but existing tests still INSERT match_decisions without kind — guaranteed 23502 regression
summary: Migration 20260516160600 drops the bridge_recommended DEFAULT on match_decisions.kind while NOT NULL is preserved (mig 080 STEP 5). bridge-outcome-cron.test.ts L134 INSERT omits `kind` entirely. After this PR lands, that INSERT raises 23502 not_null_violation and aborts the seed loop. The PR updates xor-rls + schema + outcomes-join tests with the explicit kind field but missed this file plus bridge-outcome-cron-holding.test.ts (3 INSERT sites at L237, L326, L382). Self-audit: `grep -L 'kind:' src/__tests__/bridge-outcome-cron*.test.ts` shows the regression set.
evidence: L134-L143 of bridge-outcome-cron.test.ts INSERTs allocator_id, strategy_id, decision, decided_by, original_strategy_id, original_holding_ref — no `kind:` field. Migration 20260516160600 L60-L61: `ALTER TABLE public.match_decisions ALTER COLUMN kind DROP DEFAULT;`. Test inherited DEFAULT 'bridge_recommended' previously; now it raises 23502.
fix: Add `kind: 'bridge_recommended'` to bridge-outcome-cron.test.ts L134, plus all three sites in bridge-outcome-cron-holding.test.ts (L237 holding-paired BTC, L326 ETH holding, L382 strategy-sourced). Confirm by running `grep -L 'kind:' src/__tests__/bridge-outcome-cron*.test.ts` returns nothing.

title: _assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator + visibility trigger has ZERO behavioral test coverage for the REJECTION path
summary: Migration 160700 installs a BEFORE-INSERT trigger that raises 42501 insufficient_privilege when an allocator tries to insert a voluntary_add / bridge_recommended row referencing a strategy in an organization where the allocator is NOT a member. The migration's self-verify only checks helper + trigger EXISTENCE; it does NOT exercise the REJECT path. Zero tests in supabase/tests/, src/__tests__/, or analytics-service/tests/ reference _assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator or match_decisions_visibility_check. A regression where the org lookup short-circuits to TRUE (e.g., schema-rename, SECURITY INVOKER flip, or future re-install) lands silently — bypassing the M-0825 mitigation. The task spec explicitly requests both allowed AND forbidden cases.
evidence: `grep -rn '_assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator\|_match_decisions_visibility_check\|match_decisions_visibility_check' src/__tests__/ supabase/tests/ analytics-service/tests/` returns ZERO matches. Migration 160700 self-verify L154-L184 only checks pg_proc + pg_trigger existence.
fix: Add live-DB test (src/__tests__/match-decisions-visibility-trigger.test.ts or supabase/tests/test_match_decisions_visibility_trigger.sql) covering 5 cases: (1) Strategy organization_id=NULL, allocator inserts voluntary_add → PASS. (2) Strategy in org O, allocator in O, inserts bridge_recommended → PASS. (3) Strategy in org O, allocator NOT in O, inserts voluntary_add → expect 42501 + 'visibility check' in message. (4) Orphaned org (zero members), any allocator → PASS (orphan branch). (5) voluntary_remove with NULL strategy_id → PASS (helper short-circuit on NULL strategy_id).

title: _validate_scenario_diff has zero test coverage AND is dead code (commit_scenario_batch RPC does NOT call it)
summary: Migration 160800 installs _validate_scenario_diff(jsonb, int) helper that validates per-kind required fields + percent_allocated range + UUID format and raises 22023. The migration explicitly states 'commit_scenario_batch body (from 20260515210400) is NOT mutated here' (L33) — the helper is INSTALLED but UNUSED. The smoke probe in self-verify (L195-L215) only exercises a happy-path voluntary_remove + a single invalid-kind rejection. None of the per-kind branches (voluntary_add missing strategy_id, voluntary_modify out-of-range percent_allocated, bridge_recommended missing holding_ref, non-UUID strategy_id) are exercised by any TS or SQL test. Defense-in-depth framing is hollow when nothing invokes the function AND no test pins its behavior.
evidence: `grep -rn '_validate_scenario_diff' src/__tests__/ supabase/tests/ analytics-service/tests/` returns ZERO matches. test_commit_scenario_batch_auth_input.sql Test 4 covers the EXISTING commit_scenario_batch ELSE branch — not the new helper.
fix: Either (a) wire _validate_scenario_diff into commit_scenario_batch in a follow-up migration so the existing Test 4 exercises it indirectly, OR (b) add supabase/tests/test_validate_scenario_diff.sql with 8 cases: voluntary_add missing strategy_id (22023), voluntary_add missing percent_allocated (22023), voluntary_add percent_allocated=1.5 (22023), voluntary_modify missing holding_ref (22023), bridge_recommended missing strategy_id (22023), strategy_id='not-a-uuid' (22023), kind=null (22023), kind='not_a_real_kind' (22023).

title: sanitize_user notification_dispatches purge (M-0796 core mitigation) has no behavioral test
summary: Migration 160100 adds `DELETE FROM notification_dispatches WHERE recipient_email = v_target_email;` to the sanitize_user body — the entire purpose of this migration. Verification at L255-L258 is a body-grep regex confirming the STATEMENT exists in the function definition but NOT that it executes and deletes rows. Existing src/__tests__/sanitize-user.test.ts (411 lines) does not exercise notification_dispatches at all (verified by grep). retention-crons.test.ts inserts notification_dispatches but tests the retention CRON path, not the sanitize_user path. A subtle bug (e.g., filter narrowed to status='queued') would not be caught.
evidence: `grep -rn 'notification_dispatches' src/__tests__/sanitize-user.test.ts` returns zero matches. Migration self-verify uses regex-on-body, not row-counting.
fix: Extend src/__tests__/sanitize-user.test.ts: seed auth.users + profiles with email='gdpr-test@quantalyze.test'; seed 2 notification_dispatches rows (queued + dispatched) with recipient_email='gdpr-test@quantalyze.test'; seed an unrelated email's dispatch as control; call sanitize_user(userId); assert COUNT(*) FROM notification_dispatches WHERE recipient_email='gdpr-test@quantalyze.test' = 0 AND the control row survives. Pins the M-0796 GDPR Art. 17 invariant and catches regressions to the filter scope.

title: voluntary_modify v2 CHECK rejection path uncovered + T_L1_ALL_PASS_CHECKS JS predicate is OUT OF SYNC with new v2 CHECK
summary: Migration 20260516160500 tightens the voluntary_modify CHECK to require `original_strategy_id IS NULL` (was deliberately unconstrained — H-0957/H-0961/H-0963). (a) match-decisions-schema.test.ts has no T_REJECT_VM test — no INSERT with kind='voluntary_modify' AND original_strategy_id NOT NULL exists to verify the new constraint rejects the legacy 'mixed' shape. (b) The L1 invariant predicate at L549-L553 still uses the OLD definition `(r.original_holding_ref !== null && r.strategy_id === null)` — it does NOT also require r.original_strategy_id === null. So a stale voluntary_modify row that violates the NEW SQL CHECK would PASS the JS-side L1 probe. CLAUDE.md Rule 9: tests must encode intent, not legacy behavior.
evidence: L549-L553 JS predicate: `if (r.kind === "voluntary_modify") { return !(r.original_holding_ref !== null && r.strategy_id === null); }`. Migration 160500 v2 CHECK at L72-L78 also requires `original_strategy_id IS NULL`.
fix: Two changes: (1) Add T_REJECT_VM test mirroring T_REJECT_VR / T_REJECT_VA — INSERT with kind='voluntary_modify' + original_holding_ref='holding:...:spot' + strategy_id=null + original_strategy_id=STRATEGY_PHASE10_A → expect error?.code === '23514' OR message includes 'match_decisions_kind_voluntary_modify'. (2) Update L549-L553 JS predicate to also require r.original_strategy_id === null so the L1 probe matches v2 CHECK exactly.

title: sql-tests CI job has ZERO new test files for 5 new SQL functions across 8 new migrations
summary: The PR adds 8 new migrations (20260516160000..160800) installing 5 new SQL functions/triggers (sanitize_user body change, _assert_retention_columns, _assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator, _match_decisions_visibility_check, _validate_scenario_diff) and multiple new constraints (data_deletion_requests_state_exclusive, match_decisions_kind_bridge_recommended_v2, match_decisions_kind_voluntary_modify_v2). The sql-tests CI job (ci.yml L252-L390) runs `supabase/tests/test_*.sql` — `git diff origin/main...HEAD --stat supabase/tests/` returns ZERO files changed. Existing 9 SQL self-test files cover migrations 120-128 but none reference new H-/M-issue IDs or new function names. CI passes the new migrations through silently, providing no regression signal for M-0796, M-0825, M-0826, H-0923, H-0954, H-0959.
evidence: `grep -l '_validate_scenario_diff\|_assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator\|_assert_retention_columns\|match_decisions_kind_bridge_recommended_v2\|match_decisions_kind_voluntary_modify_v2\|data_deletion_requests_state_exclusive\|H-0954\|H-0956\|H-0957\|H-0960\|M-0795\|M-0796\|M-0825\|M-0826' supabase/tests/test_*.sql` returns ZERO matches.
fix: Add at least 3 new SQL test files following existing pattern (BEGIN; DO $$ ... RAISE EXCEPTION ...; END $$; ROLLBACK): (1) test_data_deletion_requests_state_check.sql — both-terminal INSERT → 23514. (2) test_match_decisions_visibility_trigger.sql — 5 cases per finding above. (3) test_validate_scenario_diff.sql — 8 cases per finding above. Each test runs via existing `psql -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -f` loop in ci.yml L385-L387.

title: _assert_retention_columns helper installed but never invoked at runtime; no canary cron exercises drift detection
summary: Migration 160200 installs _assert_retention_columns() that asserts api_keys.is_active, profiles.email, notification_dispatches.{recipient_email,notification_type,status,created_at} exist. The migration calls it ONCE at apply time (L89-L90). After that, the helper sits installed but never invoked — the retention cron bodies themselves are NOT modified to call it. If schema drifts between deploys (column rename via Supabase platform UI, sibling-branch fork), the helper will not catch it until the next migration applies. No test asserts the helper exists OR that a hypothetical column drop raises 42703.
evidence: `grep -rn '_assert_retention_columns' src/__tests__/ supabase/tests/ analytics-service/tests/` returns zero matches. Migration L17-L28 explicitly notes 'pg_cron job bodies are stored as TEXT...not modified here'. test_retention_crons_safe.sql tests cron job bodies but not the new helper.
fix: Add supabase/tests/test_retention_columns_drift.sql with 2 cases: (1) `PERFORM public._assert_retention_columns();` → succeeds silently. (2) In a SAVEPOINT, `ALTER TABLE api_keys DROP COLUMN is_active CASCADE`, then call the helper inside BEGIN/EXCEPTION — expect SQLSTATE='42703'. ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT to restore. Pins the drift-detection invariant and makes the helper observably exercised.

title: data_deletion_requests_state_exclusive CHECK added NOT VALID — no test verifies it actually rejects new violations
summary: Migration 160000 adds a NOT VALID CHECK forbidding both completed_at AND rejected_at being NOT NULL. NOT VALID skips full-table validation but the constraint IS enforced on new INSERTs/UPDATEs. Verification at L76-L92 only checks the constraint EXISTS in pg_constraint — it does NOT exercise the rejection path. existing src/__tests__/deletion-request-admin-self.test.ts uses mocks (verified L99-L124), so it doesn't hit the live CHECK. A future migration that drops or tightens the constraint could silently regress the state-exclusivity invariant.
evidence: Migration self-verify L76-L92 checks `pg_constraint WHERE conname = 'data_deletion_requests_state_exclusive'` existence only. `grep -rn 'data_deletion_requests_state_exclusive' src/__tests__/ supabase/tests/` returns zero matches.
fix: Add supabase/tests/test_data_deletion_state_check.sql: (1) INSERT row with completed_at=now() + rejected_at=now() → expect SQLSTATE 23514 + 'data_deletion_requests_state_exclusive' in message. (2) Positive case: completed_at=now() + rejected_at=NULL succeeds. (3) Positive case: rejected_at=now() + completed_at=NULL succeeds. (4) Positive case: both NULL succeeds (pending state).

title: Stale test assertion: test_sanitize_user_hardening.sql Test 3 will FAIL post-PR (inherited regression from PR #173)
summary: test_sanitize_user_hardening.sql Test 3 asserts: `IF fn_body LIKE '%quantalyze.sanitize_in_progress%' THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'Test 3 failed (Finding 3): sanitize_user still calls set_config on the bypass GUC — Migration 127 regressed';`. Originally added when mig 127 removed the set_config call. PR #173 (mig 20260515210100 L172) RE-ADDED `PERFORM set_config('quantalyze.sanitize_in_progress', 'on', true);`. This PR's mig 160100 L60 PRESERVES the re-added call. The sql-tests CI job is therefore failing on every commit since PR #173 landed — silently masking real coverage. Task spec asks: 'Are existing tests still passing OR testing OLD behavior?' — answer: testing OLD behavior, will fail.
evidence: Migration 20260515210100 L172: `PERFORM set_config('quantalyze.sanitize_in_progress', 'on', true);`. Migration 20260516160100 L60 preserves it. Test L194-L196 still inverts the polarity. PR #173 commit 636f8ae2 did NOT update test_sanitize_user_hardening.sql.
fix: Update test_sanitize_user_hardening.sql Test 3 to assert PRESENCE not absence. Replace L194-L196 with: `IF fn_body NOT LIKE '%quantalyze.sanitize_in_progress%' THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'Test 3 failed (P911): sanitize_user lost sentinel-progress signal'; END IF;`. Also ADD: `IF fn_body NOT LIKE '%DELETE FROM notification_dispatches WHERE recipient_email%' THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'Test 3 failed (M-0796): sanitize_user lost notification_dispatches purge'; END IF;` — pins THIS PR's mitigation.

title: Empty-DB / sibling-branch apply order assumption — mig 160400/160500 require mig 080 kind column + v1 constraints
summary: Mig 160400/160500 DROP-then-ADD CHECK pattern is idempotent ONLY when mig 080 has already created the `kind` column + the v1 constraints. The idempotency probe in mig 160300 bootstraps the enum TYPE if missing but does NOT create the `kind` column on match_decisions (mig 080 STEP 6 does that). On an empty DB where mig 080 has not yet run, migrations 160400/160500 ADD CHECK statements reference NEW.kind on a column that doesn't exist → 42703 undefined_column at apply. Task asks: 'Are there integration tests verifying the migrations APPLY cleanly on an empty DB AND on a DB with the PR #173 high-hardening schema state?' — no such test exists.
evidence: Migration 160300 L46-L58 bootstraps the TYPE if missing. Migration 160400 L96-L101 references column `kind` directly. No `IF EXISTS column kind` gate.
fix: Either add a CI matrix step that applies migrations against an empty Postgres container twice (cold + re-apply idempotency probe), OR add a runtime probe inside the migration DO blocks: `IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='public' AND table_name='match_decisions' AND column_name='kind') THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'mig 160400 requires mig 080 (kind column) — apply order violated'; END IF;`. This makes a misordered apply fail loudly instead of with a cryptic 42703.

title: voluntary_modify with original_strategy_id NOT NULL: legacy rows pass NOT VALID v2 CHECK but no test detects the silent cron-coverage gap
summary: The migration RAISE NOTICEs the count of pre-existing voluntary_modify rows with original_strategy_id NOT NULL but the new v2 CHECK is added NOT VALID so apply does not abort. Operator is expected to backfill + ALTER TABLE VALIDATE separately. No test fixture seeds a violator row + asserts the cron's compute_bridge_outcome_deltas correctly attributes (or fails to attribute) it. The migration's stated risk (L11-L15) is 'voluntary_modify with NULL original_strategy_id silently picked up by compute_bridge_outcome_deltas() holding branch' — the FIX requires NULL but legacy NOT-NULL rows still exist and now fall outside every CTE branch (cron logic unchanged). Without a test, the silent-cron-skip path for legacy rows is invisible.
evidence: Migration 160500 L42-L46 counts but doesn't fail. No subsequent test asserts behavior on these rows. compute_bridge_outcome_deltas body in 20260426131718 is unchanged.
fix: Add a test in bridge-outcome-cron-holding.test.ts: seed a voluntary_modify row with original_strategy_id NOT NULL via direct admin INSERT (set session_replication_role='replica' to bypass the NOT VALID check); run compute_bridge_outcome_deltas; assert NO bridge_outcomes delta row was produced for that match_decision_id (the current silent-skip behavior). Pins the existing gap so any future cron-body fix is observable. Alternatively, emit a forensics_log row when a voluntary_modify falls outside all CTE branches and assert its existence.

title: Test fixture in test_commit_scenario_batch_auth_input.sql doesn't exercise _validate_scenario_diff helper's per-diff index annotation
summary: Test 4 'unknown kind raises 22023' tests commit_scenario_batch's existing ELSE branch. The new _validate_scenario_diff helper (mig 160800) also returns 22023 on unknown kind but with message 'commit_scenario_batch[index=%]: ...'. Because commit_scenario_batch's body was deliberately NOT modified, Test 4 still goes through the OLD code path. When the helper is wired in (the implied follow-up), Test 4 may silently keep passing because it only checks ERRCODE, not the per-diff message format — losing observability of the new index-bearing diagnostic.
evidence: test_commit_scenario_batch_auth_input.sql L210-L212: only checks `IF err_state <> '22023' THEN RAISE EXCEPTION...`. Migration 160800 L154-L155 new ELSE branch uses 'commit_scenario_batch[index=%]: unhandled kind=%'.
fix: When _validate_scenario_diff is wired into commit_scenario_batch (follow-up PR), update Test 4 to also assert message LIKE '%[index=0]%' so the per-diff index annotation is pinned. Non-blocking for THIS PR but worth flagging in the runbook for the follow-up.

title: reconstruct_unexpected_noop audit kind never tested
summary: The H-1168 branch that emits 'allocator.equity.reconstruct_unexpected_noop' when count==0 with rows in hand has NO regression test. test_h1168_reconstruct_no_data_emits_distinct_audit_kind only covers the empty-rows branch (line 1821). If a future supabase-py change or a regression in _result_row_count drops the non-zero count to zero (e.g. PostgREST stops returning data on collision), the silent-noop branch will mis-classify as reconstruct_complete or reconstruct_no_data with no test failure.
evidence: grep -n 'reconstruct_unexpected_noop' analytics-service/tests/test_equity_reconstruction.py -> no matches. Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:1818-1819 'if count == 0 and rows: audit_kind = ... reconstruct_unexpected_noop'
fix: async def test_h1168_reconstruct_unexpected_noop_when_rows_collide_to_zero(monkeypatch): seed allocator_equity_snapshots with rows that conflict on (allocator_id, asof) so the upsert returns count=0; run reconstruct; assert 'allocator.equity.reconstruct_unexpected_noop' in audit_actions and 'reconstruct_complete' NOT in actions.

title: sibling_lookup_failed audit at caller site untested
summary: C-0328 / M-1028 introduces a 'allocator.equity.sibling_lookup_failed' audit event in run_reconstruct_allocator_history_job when SiblingCheckResult.lookup_failed is True. Only the helper-level lookup_failed flag is tested (test_h1163_sibling_lookup_exception_returns_fail_safe). The caller-site emission, the behavior=fail_safe_skip_purge metadata, and the fact that purge is then SKIPPED while persist still runs are not asserted end-to-end. A refactor that silently drops the audit emission would not fail any test.
evidence: grep -n 'sibling_lookup_failed' analytics-service/tests/test_equity_reconstruction.py -> no matches. Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:1768-1779 emits audit with behavior='fail_safe_skip_purge'.
fix: async def test_c0328_sibling_lookup_failure_emits_audit_and_skips_purge(monkeypatch): wire FakeSupabase api_keys query to raise; seed stale snapshots; run reconstruct; assert audit_actions includes 'allocator.equity.sibling_lookup_failed' with behavior='fail_safe_skip_purge'; assert stale rows survived (purge was skipped); assert main audit metadata has sibling_check_failed=True.

title: ctval drift warnings 1-5% band never exercised
summary: The soft-warn band logic (drift > 1% and <= 5%) appends to ctval_drift_warnings; this entire branch and the M-1024/M-1031 observability promise has zero test coverage. PERP_AMT_CTVAL_DRIFT_WARN_THRESHOLD (0.01) and the dict shape ({raw_symbol, venue, inst_type, relative_err, amt_source}) are not asserted anywhere. A regression that flips the comparison operators or forgets to populate the list would not fail any test.
evidence: grep -n 'ctval_drift_warnings\|DRIFT_WARN_THRESHOLD' analytics-service/tests/test_equity_reconstruction.py -> only references inside doc/comment of test, no assertions on list contents. Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:947-963.
fix: def test_m1024_ctval_drift_warning_in_soft_band(): construct an OKX SWAP trade where amt*ctval and cost/price disagree by ~2% (within 1-5% band); call _compute_daily_equity with a ctval_drift_warnings=[] sink; assert sink has one entry with relative_err≈0.02, raw_symbol='ETH/USDT:USDT', amt_source=COST_DIV_PRICE.

title: perp mark price-skip C-0330 surfacing untested
summary: C-0330 was extended to perp marks at lines 1065-1068: when an OPEN perp position's price is None for the day, add base to skipped_symbols. test_c0330_skipped_symbols_are_surfaced_when_ohlcv_is_missing only exercises the spot-replay branch (line 1029-1033 region); the perp-mark surfacing is a distinct code path that mutates the same set but from a different loop. A regression that omits the perp branch (e.g. early `continue` reorder) would silently fail to surface inverted perp marks.
evidence: grep -n 'inverse_perp_symbols\|skipped_symbols' tests shows skipped_symbols is asserted only against spot 'ETH' in C-0330 test, no perp position test. Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:1064-1068.
fix: def test_c0330_perp_mark_missing_price_surfaces_via_skipped_symbols(): open a BTC/USDT:USDT long via a trade, then call _compute_daily_equity with NO OHLCV for BTC on day N (i.e. _price_on returns None during the perp mark loop); pass skipped_symbols=set() sink; assert 'BTC' in sink.

title: split_holdings_symbol_to_base_quote longest-first ordering invariant untested
summary: split_holdings_symbol_to_base_quote relies on the module-level _STABLECOINS_LONGEST_FIRST ordering to avoid USDC swallowing USD-suffix symbols. Test_h1157 only covers ETHUSDT (USDT) and BTCUSDC (USDC). It does NOT cover the actual collision case the comment warns against: a symbol where both USD AND USDC are valid suffixes — without longest-first sorting, 'BTCUSDC' would split as ('BTC', 'USD') with a 'C' orphan. The current test of ('BTC', 'USDC') only proves USDC is recognized, not that it's preferred over USD. Replacing the sorted() with a plain set iteration could produce a non-deterministic regression that passes the existing test on Python with dict-ordered sets but fails in production.
evidence: grep -n 'split_holdings_symbol_to_base_quote' analytics-service/tests/test_equity_reconstruction.py -> only two assertions (ETHUSDT, BTCUSDC). Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:54-57 + 235-237 explicitly cite the USDC-vs-USD ordering risk.
fix: def test_split_holdings_longest_first_prevents_usdc_collapse(): from services.equity_reconstruction import split_holdings_symbol_to_base_quote, _STABLECOINS_LONGEST_FIRST; assert _STABLECOINS_LONGEST_FIRST.index('USDC') < _STABLECOINS_LONGEST_FIRST.index('USD'); assert split_holdings_symbol_to_base_quote('BTCUSDC') == ('BTC', 'USDC'); assert split_holdings_symbol_to_base_quote('ETHUSDT') == ('ETH', 'USDT'); assert split_holdings_symbol_to_base_quote('') == ('', 'USDT'); assert split_holdings_symbol_to_base_quote('UNKNOWNTOKEN') == ('UNKNOWNTOKEN', 'USDT').

title: _result_row_count bool/False guard untested
summary: Stage-D red-team commit 17cec16f added isinstance(count, bool) exclusion so a stray False from a mock or older supabase-py cannot masquerade as count=0. No test covers this branch. A future refactor that drops the `not isinstance(count, bool)` clause would silently regress: `count=False` would be treated as int 0 (since False is an int subclass in Python) and the function would return 0 instead of falling through to len(data). The exact red-team failure mode is invisible to the suite.
evidence: grep -n '_result_row_count\|isinstance.*bool' analytics-service/tests/test_equity_reconstruction.py -> _result_row_count only referenced in a docstring of test_h1166. Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:1119-1125 and FIX-REPORT Stage D.
fix: def test_result_row_count_excludes_false_masquerading_as_int(): from services.equity_reconstruction import _result_row_count; class R: count = False; data = [{'a':1}, {'b':2}]; assert _result_row_count(R()) == 2  # falls through to len(data), NOT 0; class R2: count = True; data = [{'a':1}]; assert _result_row_count(R2()) == 1  # ditto for True; class R3: count = 5; data = []; assert _result_row_count(R3()) == 5  # int passes through.

title: refresh non-numeric upnl branch untested
summary: Refresh path now catches (TypeError, ValueError) when float(upnl_raw) fails and logs a warning + skips (lines 1921-1929). The test_h1161_refresh_logs_audit_when_perp_upnl_is_none covers the None branch; test_h1161_refresh_keeps_perp_breakdown_entry_when_upnl_is_zero covers 0.0. The non-numeric branch (e.g. upnl='nan', upnl='abc', upnl=[1,2,3]) has no coverage. A regression that catches a wider Exception (re-introducing the silent-swallow anti-pattern) or that fails to skip the bad row would not fail any test.
evidence: grep -n 'non-numeric\|TypeError, ValueError' analytics-service/tests/test_equity_reconstruction.py -> no matches. Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:1921-1929.
fix: async def test_h1161_refresh_skips_non_numeric_upnl(monkeypatch): seed allocator_holdings with derivative row unrealized_pnl_usd='not_a_number'; run refresh; assert outcome=DONE; assert no breakdown entry for that symbol; assert no perp_upnl_missing audit (this is a separate signal); assert refresh_complete audit was emitted (loop didn't crash).

title: FakeTable filter bypass: sibling tests pass via is_active alone, masking disconnected_at filter regression
summary: test_stale_snapshots_replaced_when_sibling_is_disconnected seeds API_KEY_ID_2 with disconnected_at=<timestamp> but NO is_active field. In the _FakeTable query, .eq('is_active', True) checks row.get('is_active')==True; an absent key returns None, which fails the equality check, so the sibling is excluded BY THE is_active FILTER, not the disconnected_at filter. If a regression silently dropped the `.is_('disconnected_at', 'null')` line from _allocator_has_other_api_keys, this test would still pass — the test no longer pins the original v0.15.3.3 fix. Mocks bypass system-under-test.
evidence: test file lines 2216-2223: fixture omits is_active and sync_status. FakeTable.execute line 215 'if not all(row.get(c) == v for c, v in self._select_filters): continue' — None != True excludes the row regardless of disconnected_at.
fix: Update test fixture to mirror production state: API_KEY_ID_2 = {'id': API_KEY_ID_2, 'user_id': ALLOCATOR_ID, 'exchange': 'okx', 'is_active': True, 'sync_status': 'ok', 'disconnected_at': '2026-04-20T12:00:00+00:00'}. With is_active=True the only remaining exclusion is disconnected_at; if that filter were dropped the sibling would be counted and the purge would be skipped (test would fail as designed).

title: telemetry surfacing into reconstruct_complete audit metadata untested
summary: reconstruct_complete now carries skipped_symbols/unknown_perp_symbols/inverse_perp_symbols/ctval_drift_warnings/sibling_check_failed in audit_metadata (lines 1834-1842) capped at [:50]. The end-to-end audit-payload contents are not asserted in any test — C-0326/9/30 tests only verify the internal sets are populated by _compute_daily_equity, not that they survive the telemetry dict→_emit_audit pipeline. A regression that drops one of these keys from the audit payload (rename mismatch, typo, accidental removal) would not fail any test.
evidence: grep -n 'skipped_symbols.*audit\|unknown_perp_symbols.*audit\|inverse_perp_symbols.*audit' analytics-service/tests/test_equity_reconstruction.py -> no matches. Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:1834-1842.
fix: async def test_telemetry_surfaces_into_reconstruct_complete_audit(monkeypatch): seed a reconstruct job where the replay produces one inverse perp fill + one unknown OKX SWAP + one OHLCV gap; inspect _emit_audit call args for 'allocator.equity.reconstruct_complete' (or _unexpected_noop); assert metadata contains keys skipped_symbols, unknown_perp_symbols, inverse_perp_symbols, ctval_drift_warnings, sibling_check_failed=False with the expected populated values.

title: OKX FUTURES dated-suffix lookup branch untested separately from generic FUTURES
summary: _resolve_perp_amt_base for FUTURES first tries OKX_FUTURES_CONTRACT_SIZE.get(raw_symbol), and if None tries the base symbol after stripping '-YYMMDD' suffix (lines 311-317). test_c0327 passes 'BTC/USDT:USDT-251226' but BTC/USDT:USDT IS in OKX_FUTURES_CONTRACT_SIZE — so the dated-symbol path actually hits the first .get() with the full raw_symbol that ISN'T in the dict... wait, line 168 builds OKX_FUTURES_CONTRACT_SIZE from OKX_PERP_CONTRACT_SIZE which has key 'BTC/USDT:USDT' (no date suffix). So in test_c0327, the first .get('BTC/USDT:USDT-251226') returns None and the second .get('BTC/USDT:USDT') hits — i.e. the suffix-strip IS exercised. But a regression that drops the suffix-strip fallback would silently return None and fall through to the cost/price path; the existing assertion would catch THAT specifically. However the case where the dated symbol is INDEPENDENTLY in the table (someone adds 'BTC/USDT:USDT-251226' directly) is not tested, and the case where suffix-strip is needed for a non-BTC/ETH symbol with a multi-segment suffix (e.g. 'XRP/USDT:USDT-251226-1') is not tested. Lower severity than initial estimate.
evidence: grep -n 'BTC/USDT:USDT-' tests -> single match (line 2838). Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:311-317.
fix: def test_c0327_futures_dated_suffix_strip_fallback(): from services.equity_reconstruction import _resolve_perp_amt_base, _PerpAmtSource, OKX_FUTURES_CONTRACT_SIZE; assert 'ETH/USDT:USDT-260327' not in OKX_FUTURES_CONTRACT_SIZE; recovered, source, _ = _resolve_perp_amt_base('ETH/USDT:USDT-260327', amount=100, price=2000, cost=100*2000.0, inst_type='FUTURES', venue='okx'); assert source == _PerpAmtSource.CTVAL_TABLE; assert recovered == pytest.approx(100 * OKX_FUTURES_CONTRACT_SIZE['ETH/USDT:USDT']).

title: _is_inverse_perp dated-FUTURES suffix tolerance untested
summary: _is_inverse_perp at line 202 has 'return settle.split("-")[0].upper() == base  # tolerate FUTURES suffix'. The dated-suffix tolerance branch (e.g. 'BTC/USD:BTC-251226' or 'ETH/USD:ETH-260327') is not asserted in any test. test_c0326_inverse_perp_returns_unsupported_sentinel only covers undated BTC/USD:BTC and ETH/USD:ETH. A regression that removes the .split('-')[0] would silently misclassify dated inverse FUTURES as linear, and cost/price values denominated in BASE would silently pollute position state — exactly the bug C-0326 was meant to close.
evidence: grep -n '_is_inverse_perp' analytics-service/tests/test_equity_reconstruction.py -> only line 2868. Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:200-202.
fix: def test_c0326_inverse_perp_dated_suffix_still_detected(): from services.equity_reconstruction import _is_inverse_perp; assert _is_inverse_perp('BTC/USD:BTC-251226') is True; assert _is_inverse_perp('ETH/USD:ETH-260327') is True; assert _is_inverse_perp('BTC/USDT:USDT-251226') is False  # linear futures still linear.

title: H-1172 logger.exception calls never asserted
summary: H-1172 added logger.exception(...) calls BEFORE sanitisation in three top-level handlers (lines 1714, 1782, 1994). The FIX-REPORT explicitly states the goal is 'full traceback reaches stdout/sentry' — but no test asserts these calls happen. A refactor that strips the logger.exception (e.g. someone 'simplifying' the redundant logging since sanitised audit already records the message) would silently delete the operational visibility. Test should encode WHY: pre-fix the audit had only sanitised 500-char message; post-fix the unredacted stack must reach the logger.
evidence: grep -n 'logger.exception\|caplog' analytics-service/tests/test_equity_reconstruction.py -> no matches for either. Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:1711-1717 + 1779-1786 + 1994-2000.
fix: async def test_h1172_unhandled_exception_logs_full_traceback(monkeypatch, caplog): caplog.set_level('ERROR'); arrange for _fetch_and_price_window to raise RuntimeError('boom with secrets'); run reconstruct; assert outcome=FAILED; assert any('boom with secrets' in r.getMessage() for r in caplog.records) and any(r.exc_info for r in caplog.records  # full traceback was logged via logger.exception); assert the audit metadata sanitised_message is bounded at 500 chars.

title: FALLBACK_AMOUNT branch (price<=0) untested
summary: _resolve_perp_amt_base returns (_PerpAmtSource.FALLBACK_AMOUNT, None) when price<=0 (lines 288-291). This branch has no test. While the caller does pre-filter price>0 in the replay loop, _resolve_perp_amt_base is a public-facing helper used by tests directly. A regression that swaps the return signature (e.g. returns 0.0 instead of amount) would silently change a contract that downstream code might rely on, and is exactly the kind of structural change red-team should catch.
evidence: grep -n 'FALLBACK_AMOUNT\|price=0\|price=-' analytics-service/tests/test_equity_reconstruction.py -> no matches. Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:288-291.
fix: def test_resolve_perp_amt_base_zero_price_returns_fallback(): from services.equity_reconstruction import _resolve_perp_amt_base, _PerpAmtSource; recovered, source, drift = _resolve_perp_amt_base('ETH/USDT:USDT', amount=10, price=0.0, cost=20000.0, inst_type='SWAP', venue='okx'); assert recovered == 10.0; assert source == _PerpAmtSource.FALLBACK_AMOUNT; assert drift is None; recovered2, source2, _ = _resolve_perp_amt_base('ETH/USDT:USDT', amount=10, price=-5.0, cost=0.0); assert source2 == _PerpAmtSource.FALLBACK_AMOUNT.

title: amt_explicit<=0 fallback branch untested
summary: _resolve_perp_amt_base has a safety fallback at line 326: 'if amt_explicit <= 0: return amt_from_cost, COST_DIV_PRICE, None'. This guard against negative-or-zero amount*ctval has no test. A regression that flips the comparison (>= instead of <=) or removes the guard could allow negative amt_base to propagate into the perp replay state, silently inverting positions.
evidence: grep -n 'amt_explicit\|amount=0\|amount=-' analytics-service/tests/test_equity_reconstruction.py -> no matches. Source: analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py:325-327.
fix: def test_resolve_perp_amt_base_negative_amount_falls_back_to_cost_div_price(): from services.equity_reconstruction import _resolve_perp_amt_base, _PerpAmtSource; recovered, source, drift = _resolve_perp_amt_base('ETH/USDT:USDT', amount=-1.0, price=2000.0, cost=-200.0, inst_type='SWAP', venue='okx'); assert source == _PerpAmtSource.COST_DIV_PRICE  # NOT CTVAL_TABLE, the negative amt_explicit guard fired; assert drift is None.

title: No CI gate enforces SHA-pin policy going forward
summary: PR #179 pins every `uses:` to a 40-char SHA, but there is NO CI step that fails when a future PR re-introduces a mutable tag pin like `actions/checkout@v4`. The workflow comments + runbook describe the convention; nothing mechanically enforces it. The next PR to add a step (or a contributor unfamiliar with C-0293) can silently re-open leg (b) of the threat model. The protection is one un-reviewed `@v<N>` away from regressing.
evidence: `grep -rE 'uses:\s*[^@]+@(v[0-9]+|main|master|latest)\b' .github/workflows/` returns 0 matches today, which confirms the pin status but no script in `scripts/` and no `frontend-policy` job step runs that grep on PRs. The `frontend-policy` job (ci.yml:118-135) runs `check-banned-packages.mjs` and `check-gdpr-export-coverage.ts` — a parallel `check-action-pins.mjs` would slot in cleanly but doesn't exist. The runbook (docs/runbooks/ci-hardening-permissions-c0293.md:104-105) only lists `actionlint` (which does not enforce SHA-pinning).
fix: Add `scripts/check-action-pins.mjs` that walks .github/workflows/*.yml and fails on any `uses:` line whose ref is not `[a-f0-9]{40}` (with allowlist for first-party reusable workflows `./...`). Wire it into the existing `frontend-policy` job alongside `check-banned-packages.mjs`. Mirror the `src/__tests__/critical-regressions.test.ts` style: a vitest test that reads each workflow YAML and asserts every uses-ref matches the SHA pattern, so the gate is one cheap unit test (no GH-Actions runner needed locally).

title: No CI gate enforces NEXT_PUBLIC-not-in-artifact contract
summary: The C-0293(c) artifact-leak fix relies on `frontend-build` using PLACEHOLDER NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_* env values UNCONDITIONALLY (ci.yml:182-189). There is NO automated check that downloads the resulting `nextjs-build` artifact and greps for `TEST_SUPABASE_URL` / `qmnijlgmdhviwzwfyzlc` / `placeholder.supabase.co`-absence. A future PR that 're-optimizes' by restoring the prior seed-aware ternary in `frontend-build` env (lines 184-185) silently re-opens the same exfil pivot. The only signal documented is a MANUAL `gh run download` + `grep` (docs/runbooks/ci-hardening-permissions-c0293.md:107-114).
evidence: No step in ci.yml after the `Upload .next + public artifact` step (line 197) re-downloads and inspects the artifact. The e2e job downloads the artifact (line 547-551) but only uses it to start `next start`; it never asserts the bundle is placeholder-only. The runbook explicitly labels its verification as a post-merge spot-check, not an in-pipeline gate. Without it, the workflow comments at lines 142-152 + 177-181 are the only barrier to regression.
fix: Add a post-upload step in `frontend-build` (or a new tiny job `frontend-build-artifact-leak-check`) that: (1) re-checks .next/static/**/*.js + .next/server/**/*.js with `grep -rE 'TEST_SUPABASE_URL|qmnijlgmdhviwzwfyzlc|sb_[a-z]{4,}_[A-Za-z0-9]+'` on the local FS BEFORE upload, expecting 0 hits; (2) explicitly verifies `https://placeholder.supabase.co` IS present (so an env-stripping regression is also caught). This costs ~1s and is the regression test the runbook claims it 'should' have.

title: Seed-gated rebuild has no contract test verifying real creds are inlined
summary: The rebuild step (ci.yml:656-662) does `rm -rf .next/server .next/static .next/build-manifest.json .next/prerender-manifest.json .next/required-server-files.json` then `npm run build`. There is NO assertion that the rebuild ACTUALLY produced a bundle containing the REAL TEST_SUPABASE_URL — the contract is 'rebuild for seed-gated specs to see real env'. If the `rm -rf` list ever drifts (e.g. Next.js emits a new manifest that overrides static at runtime), the build could short-circuit silently, the seed-gated specs would run against the placeholder bundle, and CI would still report green because the specs would self-skip via `HAS_SEED_ENV` checks. A test gap that hides a regression from BOTH the leak-fix (Path 2) and the seed-gated spec coverage simultaneously.
evidence: Step at line 670 (`Run seed-gated specs`) starts `npm run start` and runs Playwright without first verifying the rebuilt bundle. The `HAS_SEED_ENV` self-skip pattern (referenced ci.yml:697) is the only signal; if it triggers from a stale build the run still passes. No `grep` step between the rebuild and the spec run.
fix: Insert a verification step between the rebuild and the spec run (ci.yml:670): `grep -rE "$TEST_SUPABASE_URL" .next/static .next/server > /dev/null || (echo 'Rebuild did not inline TEST_SUPABASE_URL'; exit 1)`. This pins the rebuild's intent: the bundle MUST contain the real env after rebuild, else the seed-gated specs are running on the wrong artifact.

title: No Dependabot config for github-actions ecosystem to surface SHA-pin drift
summary: With every `uses:` now pinned to a SHA, there is no automated mechanism to surface upstream security/feature bumps. The review-cluster-gate report (docs/runbooks/ci-hardening-review-cluster-gate-2026-05-16.md:122-125) already notes that pinned versions trail upstream by 1-3 majors (checkout v4.3.1 vs v6.0.2; setup-node v4.4.0 vs v6.4.0; upload-artifact v4.6.2 vs v7.0.1; cache v4.3.0 vs v5.0.5). Without Dependabot, a CRITICAL advisory on a pinned action will not produce a PR — the team has to spot it manually. SHA-pinning without Dependabot trades the supply-chain attack for a stale-version drift problem, and one is silent.
evidence: `ls -la .github/` returns only `PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` + `workflows/` — no `dependabot.yml` or `dependabot.yaml`. The review-cluster doc explicitly defers Dependabot to a separate PR (line 147).
fix: Add `.github/dependabot.yml` with at minimum: `version: 2; updates: - package-ecosystem: github-actions; directory: '/'; schedule: { interval: weekly }; open-pull-requests-limit: 5; commit-message: { prefix: chore(actions) }`. Dependabot generates PRs with the new SHA + tag comment in the format this PR established, so the maintainer just reviews + merges. Stale pins surface in the security tab and the actions-update PR backlog.

title: Runbook drift: 'Verification' section presents manual checks as the only gate
summary: The runbook's Verification section lists `actionlint` + a manual `gh run download ... | grep` as the post-merge verification. It does NOT call out that NO automated CI gate enforces either contract going forward — readers may assume the manual spot-check is sufficient. The threat model table at lines 30-35 lists Pre-fix vs Post-fix state per leg, but the Post-fix entries do not flag that legs (a) and (c) currently rely on human review of the YAML diff to stay closed. The review-cluster-gate doc (separate file) acknowledges this as 4 deferred items, but a reader of just `ci-hardening-permissions-c0293.md` would not know.
evidence: Lines 102-117 describe local `actionlint` + manual `gh run download` post-merge. No 'Enforcement gaps' or 'Follow-ups' section linking to the review-cluster-gate doc's MED-deferred list. The threat-model table lines 30-35 frame all three legs as 'Post-fix state' resolved, without noting the human-review dependency.
fix: Add an 'Enforcement gaps (deferred)' subsection that points to the four MED-deferred items in ci-hardening-review-cluster-gate-2026-05-16.md (lines 143-148): SHA-pin enforcement script, artifact-content gate, Dependabot, upstream SHA force-push detection. Each entry should say 'until landed, this protection depends on human review of the YAML diff' so a future on-call reader doesn't trust the runbook as if it were a fully automated gate.

title: No vitest regression test reading workflow YAMLs for placeholder env contract
summary: The repo already has a regression-test pattern (src/__tests__/critical-regressions.test.ts) that reads source files and asserts content invariants — CRITICAL-02 'VERSION / package.json drift' is the canonical example. A parallel test for C-0293 fits this pattern exactly: read .github/workflows/ci.yml, assert (1) every uses ref matches /[a-f0-9]{40}/, (2) frontend-build env block contains literal 'https://placeholder.supabase.co' and 'placeholder' for NEXT_PUBLIC_*, (3) the rebuild step's env block uses secrets.TEST_SUPABASE_URL not placeholder. This is a $0-cost gate that fails locally and in CI before any workflow even runs, catching exactly the regressions the manual review currently has to spot.
evidence: critical-regressions.test.ts:46-52 ([CRITICAL-02]) reads VERSION + package.json and asserts equality — same shape would work for workflow YAML. grep shows no current test in src/__tests__/ that reads `.github/workflows/`.
fix: Add `[CRITICAL-C0293] CI hardening invariants` describe block to src/__tests__/critical-regressions.test.ts: (1) for each .github/workflows/*.yml, parse with js-yaml (already a transitive dep), walk every step, expect uses ref matches /^[a-f0-9]{40}$/ (or `./` for local reusables); (2) read ci.yml, expect frontend-build env block contains the literal placeholder values; (3) expect 'permissions:' appears at workflow level in all 4 files with `contents: read`. Tests fail loud at commit time, no CI runner needed to catch the regression.

title: upload-artifact `include-hidden-files: true` invariant not regression-guarded
summary: The frontend-build upload step has a defensive comment (ci.yml:207-213) explaining that `include-hidden-files: true` is critical — without it, upload-artifact@v4 silently excludes the dotfile-prefixed `.next/` directory and the e2e job crashes with 'Could not find a production build'. This is operational knowledge captured in YAML comments, not in any test. A 'cleanup' refactor that removes the option (since it 'looks like the default') would re-trigger the same silent-fail. The same regression-test pattern that covers leak prevention should also pin this invariant.
evidence: ci.yml:207-213 is comment-only. No test reads the upload-artifact step's `with:` block. The comment refers to a specific past failure mode without a corresponding test fixture.
fix: In the same `[CRITICAL-C0293]` describe block proposed above, add: `it('frontend-build upload-artifact step must set include-hidden-files: true')` that parses ci.yml and walks to the upload step's `with` map. Cheap to write, prevents a known recurring bug from re-landing.
